name: right-problem
description: Validates whether a stated problem is the problem that should be solved before solution generation begins.

purpose.problem: People often begin solving an ambiguous, symptom-led, or solution-led problem before validating the goal, obstacle, constraints, and supporting evidence.
purpose.outcome: A clear, bounded, evidence-aware problem frame with an explicit validity status and next thinking step.
inScope: Separating goals, obstacles, constraints, assumptions, and evidence
Testing whether the obstacle plausibly explains the goal gap
Identifying missing information and alternative explanations
outOfScope: Replacing domain, legal, medical, or financial expertise

thinkingHabit: Validate the problem frame before generating or selecting solutions.

requiredInputs.name: problemStatement
requiredInputs.description: The problem as currently understood by the user.

requiredInputs.name: goal
requiredInputs.description: The desired outcome that makes the problem worth solving.

requiredInputs.name: context
requiredInputs.description: Relevant environment, stakeholders, evidence, history, and boundaries.

requiredInputs.name: knownConstraints
requiredInputs.description: Known boundaries governing the feasible problem space.

validation.description: A non-empty problem statement must be provided.
validation.onFailure: Return needs clarification and request the observed undesirable condition or decision difficulty.

validation.description: A desired outcome must be distinguishable from an activity or preselected solution.
validation.condition: goal describes an outcome and is distinct from problemStatement
validation.onFailure: Return needs clarification and ask what outcome should improve.

validation.description: Materially ambiguous scope, stakeholder, timeframe, or terminology must be clarified.
validation.condition: no unresolved ambiguity can materially change the evaluation
validation.onFailure: Ask the single highest-impact clarification question.

validation.description: Goals, obstacles, constraints, assumptions, and evidence must be internally consistent.
validation.onFailure: Record the contradiction and classify the frame as incomplete or invalid.

logic.description: Separate the desired outcome from the current problem statement and any proposed solution.
logic.condition: goal, problem, and solution claims are classified
logic.onFailure: Do not proceed to solution generation.

logic.description: Identify the obstacle claimed to explain the gap between the current condition and goal.
logic.condition: at least one obstacle candidate is explicit
logic.onFailure: Request the observed factor preventing the goal.

logic.description: Test the obstacle against evidence, competing explanations, and symptom-versus-cause confusion.
logic.condition: the causal relationship is supported or its uncertainty is disclosed
logic.onFailure: Lower Logic Score and request discriminating evidence.

logic.description: Separate governing constraints from obstacles and unsupported assumptions.
logic.onFailure: Lower Constraint Score and disclose the classification gap.

logic.description: Classify the complete frame as Valid, Invalid, or Incomplete using blocking conditions and rubric evidence.
logic.condition: classification and scores are mutually consistent
logic.onFailure: Return Incomplete with the unresolved inconsistency.

criteria.name: Goal Score
criteria.description: Clarity, relevance, scope, and observability of the desired outcome.

criteria.name: Obstacle Score
criteria.description: Specificity, evidence, and causal plausibility of the identified obstacle.

criteria.name: Constraint Score
criteria.description: Completeness and correct classification of material boundaries.

criteria.name: Logic Score
criteria.description: Consistency and evidential support connecting the goal, obstacle, constraints, and conclusion.

criteria.name: Overall Thinking Score
criteria.description: Problem-frame readiness after applying blocking safeguards.

conversationRules: Reflect the current problem and goal before challenging the frame.
Ask one decision-relevant question at a time.
Prioritize the unknown most likely to change problem validity.
Use neutral Socratic questions and avoid leading the user.
Stop questioning when the frame is sufficient for the current decision.

scoreReporting.note: This mapping does not add properties to the universal output payload.

neverRules: Never generate solutions before validating the problem frame.
Never invent goals, obstacles, constraints, stakeholders, or evidence.
Never treat a proposed solution as a validated problem.
Never attribute motive, character, or capability without evidence.
Never hide a blocking ambiguity behind an overall score.
Never recommend a downstream skill whose prerequisites are unsatisfied.

nextSkill.condition: The frame is valid and governing constraints require explicit analysis.
nextSkill.reason: Define the feasible problem space before evaluating options.

nextSkill.condition: The frame is valid but too broad or complex for direct analysis.
nextSkill.reason: Decompose the validated problem into tractable components.

nextSkill.condition: The frame is valid and initial and target states are sufficiently defined.
nextSkill.reason: Identify and prioritize the differences between current and desired states.

input.problemStatement: I keep missing my morning train.
input.goal: Arrive at work by 09:00 on weekdays.

expectedOutput.nextStep: Clarify the recurring cause of late platform arrival.

input.problemStatement: We need a new CRM because sales are declining.

expectedOutput.logicalIssue: A proposed solution is presented as the problem.

input.goal: Correctly choose and explain statistical tests in assignments.

expectedOutput.nextStep: Identify the observable source of test-selection errors.

expectedOutput.logicalIssue: An unsupported people judgment is used as an obstacle.

input.problemStatement: A supported obstacle prevents a measurable goal.
Costs, quality, retention, and delivery are bad.
A measurable gap has a supported obstacle.
